description_long: This is a vintage pipe wrench-style hammer with a robust, metallic head and a wooden handle. The head features an adjustable jaw mechanism, typical of pipe wrenches, with visible gear teeth and a knurled adjustment knob. The metal head is dark, showing signs of oxidation and wear, and the handle is cylindrical, made of wood with a smooth, slightly worn finish. The transition between the metal and wood is reinforced with a metal collar. The tool is compact and sturdy, designed for gripping and turning pipes rather than traditional hammering. Its utilitarian design and visible wear suggest it has seen extensive use in urban maintenance or plumbing contexts.
description: A vintage pipe wrench with a dark, worn metal head and a smooth wooden handle, showing signs of heavy use and oxidation.
description_view_0: Front view showing the adjustable jaw and gear mechanism of the metal head, with the wooden handle extending backward.
description_view_1: Side profile highlighting the full length of the tool, the gear teeth, adjustment knob, and the cylindrical wooden handle.
description_view_2: Rear view focusing on the rounded end of the wooden handle and the back of the metal head.
description_view_3: Opposite side profile displaying the jaw, adjustment mechanism, and the handle's wood grain.
attribute_handle_type: wooden straight handle with a smooth, slightly worn finish and a metal collar at the head junction
attribute_head_type: adjustable pipe wrench head with gear teeth and a knurled adjustment knob, no traditional hammer striking face
attribute_wear_or_damage_marks: visible oxidation, rust, and surface wear on the metal head; handle shows signs of use and minor abrasions
